brief: GAP/WHY: Fabry disease (FD) is X-linked lysosomal storage disorder from alpha-Gal A (alpha-galactosidase A) deficiency, causing multiorgan globotriaosylceramide (sphingolipid) accumulation; drives ventricular hypertrophy, fibrosis, inflammation, arrhythmia, sudden death. Atrial fibrillation (AF) is common in FD but underlying cellular mechanisms are unknown. DESIGN/SETTING: Combined translational study integrating (a) clinical ECG cohort analysis, (b) novel atrial cellular FD model, (c) in silico atrial computational modeling. POPULATION/SAMPLE: Clinical cohort = 115 adults with FD across varying cardiomyopathy stages; compared with non-FD healthy controls. Cellular model = induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) genome-edited via CRISPR-Cas9 to introduce GLA p.N215S variant, differentiated into iPSC-derived atrial cardiomyocytes (iPSC-CMs); compared to wild-type (WT) iPSC-CMs. INTERVENTION/COMPARISON: ECG P-wave characteristics FD vs controls; GLA p.N215S iPSC-CMs vs WT for contraction, calcium handling, electrophysiology; bi-atrial in silico models built with the cellular changes from GLA p.N215S iPSC-CMs. RESULTS: (1) ECG in FD adults showed P-wave duration shortening and PQ interval shortening BEFORE onset of cardiomyopathy. (2) FD patients had higher incidence of premature atrial contractions and increased risk of AF vs healthy controls. (3) GLA p.N215S iPSC-CMs were deficient in alpha-Gal A and showed globotriaosylceramide accumulation. (4) Atrial GLA p.N215S iPSC-CMs vs WT: more positive diastolic membrane potential, faster action potential upstroke velocity, greater incidence of delayed afterdepolarizations, greater contraction force, and altered calcium handling. (5) In silico models incorporating these cellular changes reproduced P-wave morphology changes seen in early FD cardiomyopathy and showed increased AF vulnerability. (No p-values, CIs, or effect-size statistics reported in abstract.) TAKEAWAY: Early atrial remodeling at the cellular level drives atrial arrhythmia in FD; provides mechanistic basis for early P-wave changes; identifies potential therapeutic targets to reduce arrhythmic burden in FD.
